mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is above a restrictive threshold, higher borrowing costs for corporations and higher discount rates for future earnings compress equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward information coefficient (IC) is positive over a 3-year rolling window, or triggered instances do not show statistically significant underperformance versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust and expanding economy. This strength translates into higher corporate earnings expectations, driving positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: A positive 6-month change in PAYEMS is followed by negative S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: In a high interest rate environment, a significant downturn in housing starts (HOUST) acts as a leading indicator for a broader economic slowdown, as it is one of the most rate-sensitive sectors. This signals lower future growth and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The specified trigger condition, when met, is not followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of instances during a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in Industrial Production (INDPRO) indicates that the supply side of the economy is expanding, meeting demand and generating strong corporate revenues. This fundamental strength supports higher equity prices.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate (UNRATE) combined with restrictive monetary policy (high FEDFUNDS) creates fears of wage-push inflation and a 'higher for longer' Fed stance. This interaction signals headwinds for equities as policy is unlikely to ease.
failure_condition: Instances where UNRATE < 5.0% and FEDFUNDS > 4.5% are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) erodes the real value of future corporate earnings and forces the central bank to maintain a restrictive policy stance, both of which are detrimental to equity market performance.
failure_condition: The strategy of shorting the S&P 500 when the trigger is active fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio over a 5-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong short-term momentum in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer spending, which directly boosts revenues for a large portion of S&P 500 companies, leading to positive near-term stock performance.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 1-month change in RSAFS and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate is a classic, high-confidence signal of an impending recession. This sharp deterioration in the labor market leads to a broad-based sell-off in equities as future earnings estimates are revised down.
failure_condition: In more than 40% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 posts a positive return over the subsequent 60 trading days.
